title: Apply urgently for a permanent resident card or permanent resident travel document - Canada.ca
content:
## Apply urgently for a permanent resident card or permanent resident travel document
Depending on your situation, you may be eligible to apply urgently for either
- a permanent resident (PR)  card or
- a permanent resident travel document (PRTD)
Answer the questions below to find out if you’re eligible.

## Tell us about your situation
To be eligible for urgent processing , you must show that your need the PR card or PRTD to travel for one of these reasons:
- work related to your current job
- your own serious illness
- the serious illness or death of a family member
- the loss or theft of your PR card while you were temporarily outside Canada
- a crisis, emergency or vulnerable situation you are in
- other reasons that an officer may deem legitimate (for PRTD applications only)

## I’m leaving Canada in less than 3 weeks, and I haven’t   applied for a PR card yet.
We can’t process a PR card application in less than  3 weeks. However, you can apply urgently for a PRTD once
- you’re outside Canada and
- your flight back to Canada is leaving within the next 5 days
A PRTD lets you re-enter Canada if you don’t have a valid PR card.
Without a PR card or a PRTD, you can travel to Canada only by land using  a private vehicle , like a car.

### How to apply urgently for a PRTD
- Prepare your PRTD application (opens in a new tab) .
- Include all these documents with your application: proof of travel that shows your destination and travel dates, such as your itinerary a copy of your ticket a copy of the receipt for your trip that shows the date amount paid payment method a letter that explains the reason for the urgency proof of the urgency, such as a doctor’s note death certificate letter from your employer if applicable, English or French translations of your documents
- proof of travel that shows your destination and travel dates, such as your itinerary a copy of your ticket
- a copy of your ticket
- a copy of the receipt for your trip that shows the date amount paid payment method
- a letter that explains the reason for the urgency
- proof of the urgency, such as a doctor’s note death certificate letter from your employer
- letter from your employer
- if applicable, English or French translations of your documents
- Check that your documents are clear and easy to read . If they aren’t, we’ll need to ask you to resubmit your documents. This will delay your application.
- If they aren’t, we’ll need to ask you to resubmit your documents. This will delay your application.
- Tell us that your application is urgent. If you’re applying online , check “ Yes, my request is urgent ” in the Permanent Residence Portal. If you need to apply on paper , write “ urgent ” on the envelope.
- If you’re applying online , check “ Yes, my request is urgent ” in the Permanent Residence Portal.
- If you need to apply on paper , write “ urgent ” on the envelope.

### How we process your application
We’ll review your application. If you forget to include proof of urgency, or if your situation doesn’t qualify for urgent processing, we’ll process your application normally.
- Find out what happens after you apply for a PRTD (opens in a new tab) .
- When you return to Canada, apply for your PR card (opens in a new tab) .
Even if you’re eligible for urgent processing, we can’t guarantee that you’ll get your PR card or travel document on time .
- You can check the current processing times for PR cards .
- We don’t currently post processing times for PRTDs. However, we prioritize the processing of these applications.

We can’t process a PR card application in  less than 3 weeks. However, you can apply urgently for a PRTD once
- you’re outside Canada and

A PRTD lets you re-enter Canada if you don’t have a valid PR card.

- Tell us that your application is urgent. If you’re applying online, check “ Yes, my request is urgent ” in the Permanent Residence Portal. If you need to apply on paper , write “ urgent ” on the envelope.

- If you need to apply on paper , write “ urgent ” on the envelope.

- Provide a detailed explanation of what happened to your PR card.
If you don’t want to reapply for a new PR card when you return to Canada, complete a Solemn Declaration form (IMM 5451) to report the loss of your card.
Even if you’re eligible for urgent processing, we can’t guarantee that you’ll get your PR card or travel document on time .

## I’m leaving Canada in more than 3 weeks, and I haven’t applied for a PR card
You can apply urgently for a PR card. Follow these steps:
- Prepare your PR card application (opens in a new tab) .
- Include all these documents with your application: proof of travel that shows your destination and travel dates, such as your itinerary a copy of your ticket a copy of the receipt for your trip that shows the date amount paid payment method a letter that explains the reason for the urgency proof of the urgency, such as a doctor’s note death certificate letter from your employer if applicable, English or French translations of your documents
- proof of travel that shows your destination and travel dates, such as your itinerary a copy of your ticket
- a copy of your ticket
- a copy of the receipt for your trip that shows the date amount paid payment method
- a letter that explains the reason for the urgency
- proof of the urgency, such as a doctor’s note death certificate letter from your employer
- letter from your employer
- if applicable, English or French translations of your documents
- Check that your documents are clear and easy to  read . If they aren’t, we’ll need to ask you to resubmit your documents. This will delay your application.
- If they aren’t, we’ll need to ask you to resubmit your documents. This will delay your application.
- Tell us that your application is urgent. If you’re applying online, check “ Yes, my request is urgent ” in the Permanent Residence Portal. If you need to apply on paper , write “ urgent ” on the envelope.
- If you’re applying online, check “ Yes, my request is urgent ” in the Permanent Residence Portal.
- If you need to apply on paper , write “ urgent ” on the envelope.

### How we  process your application
We’ll review  your application. If you forget to include proof of urgency, or if your situation  doesn’t qualify for urgent processing, we’ll process your application normally.
When your card is ready, we usually mail it to you. Sometimes, we may ask you to pick it up at a local IRCC office.
If you don’t get your PR card before you travel, find out how to return to Canada without a PR card (opens in a new tab) .
Find out what happens after you apply for a PR card (opens in a new tab) .
Even if you’re eligible for urgent processing, we can’t guarantee that you’ll get your PR card or travel document on time .
- You can check the current processing times for PR cards .
- We don’t currently post processing times for PRTDs. However, we prioritize the processing of these applications.

## I’m leaving Canada in more than 3 weeks, and I recently applied for a PR card.
Check your application status online (opens in a new tab) .

### If your application is already in process
- Go to our web form (opens in a new tab) .
- Include your email address under “Enquirer information”.
- Upload all these documents in the web form: proof of travel that shows your destination and travel dates, such as your itinerary a copy of your ticket a copy of the receipt for your trip that shows the date amount paid payment method a letter that explains  the reason for the urgency proof of urgency, such as a doctor’s note death certificate letter from your employer if applicable, English or French translations of your documents
- proof of travel that shows your destination and travel dates, such as your itinerary a copy of your ticket
- a copy of your ticket
- a copy of the receipt for your trip that shows the date amount paid payment method
- a letter that explains  the reason for the urgency
- proof of urgency, such as a doctor’s note death certificate letter from your employer
- letter from your employer
- if applicable, English or French translations of your documents
- Check that your documents are clear and easy to  read . If they aren’t, we’ll need to ask you to resubmit  your documents. This will delay your application.
- If they aren’t, we’ll need to ask you to resubmit  your documents. This will delay your application.
- Submit the web form.

### After you submit the web form
We’ll review the information and documents you sent. If your situation doesn’t qualify for urgent processing, we’ll process your  application normally.
When your card is ready, we usually mail it to you. Sometimes, we may ask you to pick it up at a local IRCC office.

### If your application isn’t showing in the application status tool
If your application isn’t showing, and you haven’t received an acknowledgement of receipt, follow the steps below to submit a new  application.
- Prepare a new PR card application (opens in a new tab) . You must resubmit all documents  with the new application, such as another set of photos all your supporting documents certified copies of documents, if applicable
- You must resubmit all documents  with the new application, such as another set of photos all your supporting documents certified copies of documents, if applicable
- another set of photos
- all your supporting documents
- certified copies of documents, if applicable
- You also need to include all these documents: proof of travel that shows your destination and travel dates, such as your itinerary a copy of your ticket a copy of the receipt for your trip  that shows the date amount paid payment method a letter that explains the reason for the urgency proof of the urgency, such as a doctor’s note death certificate letter from your employer if applicable, English or French translations of your documents
- proof of travel that shows your destination and travel dates, such as your itinerary a copy of your ticket
- a copy of your ticket
- a copy of the receipt for your trip  that shows the date amount paid payment method
- a letter that explains the reason for the urgency
- proof of the urgency, such as a doctor’s note death certificate letter from your employer
- letter from your employer
- if applicable, English or French translations of your documents
- Check that your documents are clear and easy to read . If they aren’t, we’ll need to ask you to resubmit  your documents. This will delay your application.
- If they aren’t, we’ll need to ask you to resubmit  your documents. This will delay your application.
- Tell us that your application is urgent. If you’re applying online, check “ Yes, my request is urgent ” in the Permanent Residence Portal. If you need to apply on paper , write “ urgent ” on the envelope.
- If you’re applying online, check “ Yes, my request is urgent ” in the Permanent Residence Portal.
- If you need to apply on paper , write “ urgent ” on the envelope.

### How we process your application
We’ll review your application. If you forget to include proof of urgency, or if your situation doesn’t qualify for urgent processing, we’ll process your application normally.
When your card is ready, we  usually mail it to you. Sometimes, we may ask you to pick it up at a local IRCC office.
If you don’t receive your PR  card before you travel, find out how to how to return to Canada without a PR card (opens in a new tab) .
Find out what happens after you apply for a PR card (opens in a new tab) .
Even if you’re eligible for urgent processing, we can’t guarantee that you’ll get your PR card or travel document on time .
- You can check the current processing times for PR cards .
- We don’t currently post processing times for PRTDs. However, we prioritize the processing of these applications.

## I’m leaving Canada in more than 3 weeks, and I can’t find my PR card.
You can apply urgently for a PR card. Follow these steps:
- Prepare your PR card application (opens in a new tab) .
- Fill out section 6 of your application (details concerning a lost, stolen, damaged or never-received PR card). Provide a detailed explanation of what happened to your PR card.
- Provide a detailed explanation of what happened to your PR card.
- Include all these documents with your application: proof of travel that shows your destination and travel dates, such as your itinerary a copy of your ticket a copy of the receipt for your trip that shows the date amount paid payment method a letter that explains  the reason for the urgency proof of urgency, such as a doctor’s note death certificate letter from your employer if applicable, English or French translations of your documents

- letter from your employer
- if applicable, English or French translations of your documents
- Check that your documents are clear and easy to  read .
- If they aren’t, we’ll need to ask you to resubmit your documents. This will delay your application.
- Tell us that your application is urgent. If you’re applying online, check “ Yes, my request is urgent ” in the Permanent Residence Portal. If you need to apply on paper , write “ urgent ” on the envelope.
- If you’re applying online, check “ Yes, my request is urgent ” in the Permanent Residence Portal.
- If you need to apply on paper , write “ urgent ” on the envelope.

### How we process your application

Find out what happens after you apply for a PR card (opens in a new tab) .
Even if you’re eligible for urgent processing, we can’t guarantee that you’ll get your PR card or travel document on time .

- We don’t currently post processing times for PRTDs. However, we prioritize the processing of these applications.

## I’m outside Canada, and I don’t have my valid PR card with me
You can’t apply for a PR card outside Canada.
You can apply urgently for a permanent resident travel document (PRTD) if you’re travelling back to Canada within the next 5 days .
- A PRTD will let you re-enter Canada if you don’t have a PR card.
- Without a PR card or a PRTD, you can travel to Canada only by land in a private vehicle, like a car.

### How to apply urgently for a PRTD
- Prepare your PRTD application (opens in a new tab) .
- Include all these documents with your application: proof of travel that shows your destination and travel dates, such as your itinerary a copy of your ticket a copy of the receipt for your trip that shows the date amount paid payment method a letter that explains  the reason for the urgency proof of urgency, such as a doctor’s note death certificate letter from your employer if applicable, English or French translations of your documents
- proof of travel that shows your destination and travel dates, such as your itinerary a copy of your ticket
- a copy of your ticket
- a copy of the receipt for your trip that shows the date amount paid payment method
- a letter that explains  the reason for the urgency
- proof of urgency, such as a doctor’s note death certificate letter from your employer
- letter from your employer
- if applicable, English or French translations of your documents
- Check that your documents are clear and easy to  read . If they aren’t, we’ll need to ask you to resubmit your documents. This will delay your application.
- If they aren’t, we’ll need to ask you to resubmit your documents. This will delay your application.
- Tell us that your application is urgent. If you’re applying online , check “ Yes, my request is urgent ” in the Permanent Residence Portal. If you need to apply on paper , write “ urgent ” on the envelope.
- If you’re applying online , check “ Yes, my request is urgent ” in the Permanent Residence Portal.
- If you need to apply on paper , write “ urgent ” on the envelope.

### How we process your application
We’ll review your application. If you forget to include proof of urgency, or if your situation doesn’t qualify for urgent processing, we’ll process your application normally.
- Find out what happens after you apply for a PRTD (opens in a new tab) .
- When you return to Canada, apply for your PR card (opens in a new tab) .
Even if you’re eligible for urgent processing, we can’t guarantee that you’ll get your PR card or travel document on time .
- You can check the current processing times for PR cards .
- We don’t currently post processing times for PRTDs. However, we prioritize the processing of these applications.

## You are not eligible for urgent processing.
You can follow the regular process to apply for
- a PR card (if you’re in Canada)
- a PRTD (if you’re outside Canada without a valid PR card)

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
